title: HTTP Communication Issue between Podman Containers in the same Network
body:
### Issue Description

The setup is for 2 Containers (Client and Server) under the same Network using Podman. 
We are trying to perform inter-container communication using HTTP Requests.
The Client container is running a python script that uses urllib which tries to send a HTTP Request to the Server Container.
Current issue is that the Client container encounters the following error: **Temporary failure in name resolution**

The same setup was created using Docker and it works perfectly. 
Also, the same setup was used but using Socket Communication instead of HTTP communication and it works for either Podman rootful or rootless containers.

### Steps to reproduce the issue:
### (1) For the Server and Client, the following files are used:
**Server**
_index.html_: file used to send as response to Client upon HTTP request
![0_nano_index_html](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218048334-74c8c00d-e4bc-4cf4-aea9-01bbb9799aa1.JPG)

_server.py_: script used to create and start the Web Server
![2_nano_server_py](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218048473-503a63a5-289c-400c-9cda-0bc12882f4a1.JPG)

_Dockerfile_: file used to build the podman image for the Server Container
![3_nano_Dockerfile](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218048543-0497bd9f-4b63-46b8-af58-6832e9602b75.JPG)

**Client** 
_client.py_: script used to send request to Web Server, which in turn will acquire the response from the Server
![2_nano_client_py](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218049832-794bbb7d-3d39-4934-afd3-f3389d5d04db.JPG)

_Dockerfile_: file used to build the podman image for the Client Container
![3_nano_Dockerfile](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218050424-ebdde9d1-6316-432a-9728-23d979cdd8ac.JPG)
_____________________________________________________________________________


###  (2) The overall environment was created with the following commands:
**(2.1)**
 To begin, the network is created in which the 2 containers (Client and Server) will operate.
`sudo podman create --name=test_server --network=test-network --ip=172.18.0.2 --network-alias test_server -p 1234:1234 --restart always test_server:00.00.01 python ./server.py`

Here is the network configuration of the network created:
![3_podman_network_inspect_test-network](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218051396-8764ca1a-6f42-431c-ba5b-135c470983a3.JPG)


**(2.2)**
Afterwards, the images are built for the containers of the Server and Client.
_Server_
![4_podman_build_test_server](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218058309-64c75e3a-2bce-49ad-bef7-58d429393537.JPG)

_Client_
![4_podman_build_test_client](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218051628-876b4751-9059-44bb-bd90-46b5a939030c.JPG)


**(2.3)**
The containers are then built for the Server and the Client using the Images built from the previous step.
_Server_
`sudo podman create --name=test_server --network=test-network --ip=172.18.0.2 --network-alias test_server -p 1234:1234 --restart always test_server:00.00.01 python ./server.py`

_Client_
`sudo podman create --name=test_client --network=test-network --ip=172.18.0.3 --network-alias test_client --restart always test_client:00.00.01 python ./client.py`

**(2.4)**
Afterwards, 2 terminals are opened to attach to each containers and the following error was encountered on the Client Container:
_Server_
![6_podman_start_test_server](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218052509-4b3f165e-cab9-4b01-9945-ff77722fd8db.JPG)

_Client_
![6_podman_start_test_client](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218052466-8cca5e77-efed-478b-a11e-7771f3cae13c.JPG)


### Describe the results you received

The Client container once executed encounters an error: **Temporary failure in name resolution**
![6_podman_start_test_client](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218053124-66ba4edb-ba71-4d3a-b280-440b3e57b757.JPG)


### Describe the results you expected

The Client container should not encounter an error and should run smoothly, providing the message shown below which comes as the response from the Server container:
![6_podman_start_test_client_OK](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218054241-54566f84-ddd3-4627-a1f5-edf5b7f9573b.JPG)


### podman info output


### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details: 

(1) Virtual Machine (VirtualBox) - Ubuntu 20.04 LTS
> $ cat /etc/os-release 
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="20.04.5 LTS (Focal Fossa)"

### Additional information

For additional reference, we've provided the interface configuration below:
![4_ifconfig](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218052747-1aadb0e2-3256-45bc-b4cc-84d5fc0c4b94.JPG)

comments.body: Sorry we cannot support 3.4, it is just way to old at this point. I suggest you update to the latest podman version with the new network stack netavark and test if this works better for you.

> Sorry we cannot support 3.4, it is just way to old at this point. I suggest you update to the latest podman version with the new network stack netavark and test if this works better for you.

Hello @Luap99! Thank you for the feedback. I just have some questions:
1. Regarding the update, does this mean I should update my current VM Machine from Ubuntu 20.04 to Ubuntu 22.04 as well?

AFAIK ubuntu 22.04 still only has podman v3.4.4.
I don't really care what distro you use or how you get podman. As upstream maintainer we simply do not have time to deal with these things. The netavark stack contains a completely new dns server called aardvark-dns which fixed a lot of dns problems.

I understand. I'll try the latest podman version and close this issue once I've finished confirming that this bug no longer occurs when using the latest podman version.

Thank you very much!

Hello @Luap99 ! We tried the same setup only this time we are using the latest version of podman: ![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/94945936/218729952-81f30b88-238e-4897-9a74-4701bfc9fdda.png) From the previous setup, we encountered the following error: > Temporary failure in name resolution However, for the current setup in using the latest podman version, we encountered the following error: We tried changing the hostname being used by the client to communicate to the server and encountered the underlying URL errors when the Client container is started: - http://172.18.0.1:1234/ : 503 Service Unavailable - http://172.18.0.2:1234/ : 503 Service Unavailable - http://localhost:1234/ : 403 Forbidden  We also tried using the following command which connects the Client container to the Network being used: `sudo podman network connect test-network test_client` The same errors were encountered as well upon the use of the aforementioned command.
Hello @Luap99! Can we have any feedback about this one? Thank you!
Sorry I don't really understand what you are doing. If you get HTTP responses then as far as podman is concerned networking is working but your application seems to respond with errors.

This is a bit weird that the application is not working, considering that it works when docker is used instead of podman. We will check this further on our end. 

Thank you for the feedback @Luap99!
Hello @Luap99! Is it ok to post this in the Discussion Section as well? Maybe others will have an idea about it.

Hello @Luap99! I am closing this ticket and opened another ticket in the Discussion Section (Q&A).
You can refer to the new ticket here:
[https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/17637](https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/17637)
